titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 46

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=148 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:03.893222+00 | translation moa=149 status=live modified=2016-06-07 14:44:15.970612+00

p.cl:
Vniuersis Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis ad quos presens scriptum peruenerit . Henricus . Reuel ⁊ Margareta
sponsa eius . Salutem . In domino .

nos dedisse ⁊ concessisse ⁊ hac nostra presenti
Carta confirmasse deo ⁊ Ecclesie Sancti Andree apostoli in Scotia . ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo
seruientibus ⁊ seruituris in puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam terram que iacet a Cultrath
uersus aquilonem . ⁊ a via qua itur a Balmurinach ad Cultrath versus Occidentem . Sicut
ego predictus Henricus Reuel . ⁊ Ricardus Reuel nepos meus ⁊ Matheus Canonicus cum probis
hominibus meis eam perambulauimus pro Quindecim acris cum communi pastura ad tantam terram
pertinente pro anima mea ⁊ anima margarete sponse mee . ⁊ pro animabus antecessorum ⁊ succes
sorum nostrorum . Qvare volumus ut predicti Canonici predictam terram habeant ⁊ possideant ita
libere ⁊ quiete ab omni seruitio ⁊ exactione seculari  sicut aliquam terram liberius ⁊ quietius tenent
⁊ possident in toto regno Scotie .

Hiis testibus . Ricardo Reuel . Iocio de Balendart . Radulfo
capello . Adam de Ardift . Roberto filio Arnaldi . Roberto filio Galfridi . Odone de Corbri . Mau
ricio Clerico . Adam Bruneie . ⁊ multis aliis .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church to whom the present written deed shall come, Henry Revel and Margaret his spouse send Greeting in the Lord:

we have given, and granted, and by this our present charter made firm, to God and to the Church of St Andrew the apostle in Scotia, and to the Canons in that place serving God now and in the future, in pure and perpetual alms, the land that lies from Coultra northwards, and from the the road that goes from Balmerino to Coultra westwards, just as I, the aforesaid Henry Revel, and Rcihard Revel my nephew, and Matthew the Canon, with my worthy men, perambulated for fifteen acres, with the common pasture that belongs to so much land, for my soul and the soul of Margaret my spouse, and for the souls of our ancestors and successors. Wherefore we will that the aforesaid Canons have and possess the aforesaid land as free and quit from every secular service and exaction, just as they hold and possess any land free and quit in the whole of the kingdom of Scotland.
